input: Stakeholder message: 'Hey, we need a landing page for the new product. It should look clean and modern and convert well. The audience is like, busy professionals. We want it done asap — maybe by end of month? Budget is flexible. Oh and make sure it works on mobile. Let me know if you have questions.' No additional context provided.
rubric: forces clarification on: product name and what it does
forces clarification on: specific audience definition (job title, company size, pain point)
forces clarification on: primary conversion goal (signup, demo, purchase, email capture)
forces clarification on: deadline (end of which month, and is that copy-complete or live?)
extracts whatever is inferable without fabricating: mobile requirement confirmed, clean aesthetic noted
output matches a structured template with labeled fields: Goal, Audience, Metric, Constraint, Deadline, Open Questions
does not invent requirements not stated or inferable

input: Email thread excerpt: 'For the Q3 campaign we are thinking social + email. Budget is $40k. We have the brand assets already. Approvals will be fast this time lol. Target is existing customers who haven't bought in 90 days. We want to drive reactivation. Can you start Monday? The legal team needs to review anything with a discount offer. Deadline is firm — August 15.' Product is a SaaS tool; no further product details given.
rubric: extracts: goal = reactivation of lapsed customers (90-day window)
extracts: audience = existing customers, lapsed 90+ days
extracts: budget = $40k
extracts: constraint = legal review required for discount offers
extracts: deadline = August 15 (firm)
forces clarification on: what KPI defines reactivation (login, purchase, renewal?)
forces clarification on: is a discount offer planned, or is that a contingent constraint?
does not fabricate product details or campaign tactics not mentioned

input: Slack message: 'We should do a case study on Meridian. They are a great customer and have good results. Marketing can write it. It needs to go on the website and maybe a PDF version too. Probably 1-2 pages. We want to use it in sales. Can someone own this?' No metrics, no quotes, no timeline, no approval chain given.
rubric: forces clarification on: what specific results/metrics can Meridian share (% improvement, dollar figure, time saved)
forces clarification on: has Meridian approved participation and quote usage?
forces clarification on: who owns this (a name, not just 'marketing')
forces clarification on: deadline and which sales cycle this needs to support
extracts: format = web page + PDF, length ~1-2 pages, use case = sales enablement
does not invent metrics or fabricate a Meridian story
output template includes: Goal, Audience (for the case study), Metric, Constraint, Deadline, KPIs, Open Questions

input: Brief document (partially filled): 'Project: Rebrand the mobile app. Objective: Make it feel more premium. Timeline: 6 weeks. Stakeholders: Product, Design, Marketing. Out of scope: backend changes. Notes: CEO saw a competitor's app and wants something similar. The competitor is Notion.' Fields left blank: Success Metric, Target Users, Budget, Approval Process.
rubric: flags that 'feel more premium' is not a measurable objective — forces clarification on success metric
flags that 'similar to Notion' is ambiguous — forces clarification on which specific elements (IA, visual style, interactions, all three?)
forces clarification on budget (designer hours? external agency?)
forces clarification on approval process — who signs off, and at what stages?
extracts confirmed constraints: backend out of scope, 6-week timeline, three stakeholder groups
does not invent a rebrand direction or success metric
output template is complete with all inferable fields filled and blank fields clearly marked as open questions

input: Request from head of growth: 'We want to A/B test the homepage. Run the test for like 2-3 weeks. Hypothesis: if we change the hero headline to be more benefit-focused, we will get more signups. The current headline is Manage your projects in one place. We need a stat sig result. Okay to use Optimizely. Just don't change the design, only copy. Owner: growth team.' No variant copy provided. No traffic volume or current conversion rate given.
rubric: forces clarification on: variant headline copy — what is the proposed benefit-focused alternative?
forces clarification on: current traffic to homepage and baseline conversion rate (needed to determine if 2-3 weeks is sufficient for stat sig)
forces clarification on: what is the primary metric (signup clicks, completed signups, trial starts?)
forces clarification on: what stat sig threshold — 90%, 95%, or other?
extracts confirmed: tool = Optimizely, constraint = copy only, owner = growth team, duration target = 2-3 weeks
does not invent variant copy or assume traffic volume
output template includes Goal, Audience, Metric, Constraint, Deadline, KPIs, Open Questions

input: Forwarded email from sales: 'Hey can marketing make us a one-pager for enterprise deals? We keep losing to [COMPETITOR REDACTED] because they have better collateral. Needs to cover our security certs, integrations, and pricing. We need it ASAP. SLA and uptime stuff too. Attach it as PDF. Make it look sharp.' Attached: nothing. No template, no brand guide reference, no specific competitor information, no current win/loss data.
rubric: forces clarification on: which specific security certifications (SOC 2? ISO 27001? HIPAA?)
forces clarification on: which integrations to feature (full list or top 5?)
forces clarification on: is pricing included (enterprise pricing is often confidential in collateral)
forces clarification on: what does 'ASAP' mean in a deliverable context — days, weeks?
extracts confirmed: format = PDF one-pager, use case = enterprise sales cycle, must cover SLA/uptime
does not invent certification names, integration names, or pricing tiers
output template marks each open question as blocking (cannot write) vs. non-blocking (can write around it)
